item.title: The World Has Forgotten Sudan, but I Haven’t
item.description: Despite three years of war, the spirit of the Sudanese people cannot be crushed.

item.title: A.I.: Creativity Killer or Companion?
item.description: Readers respond to a column by Ezra Klein. Also: Reflections on Pope Leo XIV; the healing power of nurses in the I.C.U.

item.title: Why American Jews Still Weep for Willy Loman
item.description: A revival of “Death of a Salesman” comes as the Zionist consensus is openly fracturing.

item.title: A Paris Court Just Rewrote the Rules of Corporate Morality
item.description: The profit motive was on trial. The verdict was scathing.

item.title: Section 702 Lets the Government Spy on Americans. Congress Must Fix It.
item.description: It’s time to fix a crucial but flawed tool that has allowed the government to violate Americans’ constitutional rights.

item.title: Our Tax System Should Make You Furious
item.description: The tax expert Ray Madoff explains why the American tax system is broken, and how to make it fairer.

item.title: Three Questions for Kevin Warsh, Trump’s Fed Pick
item.description: Senators must assess whether Mr. Warsh would perform his duties as a public servant, or whether he would serve the interests of the president.

item.title: It’s the 1970s Again. Democrats Should Study Up on Reagan.
item.description: Good politicians play the hand they’re dealt.

item.title: When Good Grammar Gets Snobbish
item.description: Rules don’t always lead to good sense.

item.title: We Are Gambling Away Our Future
item.description: America’s view of liberty is upside down.

item.title: I Almost Never Predict Supreme Court Outcomes. Trump Will Lose This Case.
item.description: This immigration case is really about procedure.

item.title: Orban Lost. But Populism Is Very Much Alive.
item.description: Critics of populists need to do more than thrive off the missteps of their opponents.

item.title: Why Jeff Bezos’ Tax Rate Is Lower Than Yours
item.description: The tax expert Ray Madoff explains why the American tax system is broken, and how to make it fairer.

item.title: Hillary Clinton’s Plan to Help Families
item.description: Readers respond to “How to Fix Affordability,” an Opinion guest essay by Hillary Clinton. Also: President Trump’s arch; protests and litigation.

item.title: The Second Coming of Donald Trump
item.description: Who doesn’t get the president mixed up with Jesus?

item.title: This Is Where Donald Trump Loses
item.description: President Trump won’t be able to defy the courts if he loses his Supreme Court case on birthright citizenship, which he is likely to do, the conservative court watcher Sarah Isgur argues on “Interesting Times.”

item.title: The Failure of Donald Trump
item.description: President Trump’s efforts to rule through executive order on issues like tariffs and birthright citizenship have largely failed — and will continue to fail — the conservative court watcher Sarah Isgur argues on “Interesting Times.”

item.title: Don’t Use A.I. to Do This
item.description: People are usually surprised when I admit that I love A.I.

item.title: Trump Is the End of a 100-Year Experiment
item.description: A conservative court watcher explains why the president has failed to bend the judicial branch to his will.

item.title: Older Women Are in Demand by Younger Men
item.description: What a shift in the dating preferences of younger men reveals about our changing norms.

item.title: Trump Is the End of a 100-Year Experiment
item.description: A conservative court watcher explains why the president has failed to bend the judicial branch to his will.

item.title: Eric Swalwell, Tony Gonzales and the Enduring Relevance of #MeToo
item.description: Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales are now being held accountable thanks in part to the feminism of the 2010s.

item.title: Why Young Men Want to Be With Older Women
item.description: Age-gap dating is everywhere right now — especially younger men seeking out older women. On “The Opinions,” men share why they’re drawn to the maturity, stability and clarity they can’t find elsewhere.

item.title: When Older Women Date Younger Men
item.description: There’s a growing trend of younger men dating older women — and unlike the backlash older men face, this shift can be perceived as women reclaiming their power, Emily Leibert argues on “The Opinions.”

item.title: Can a Bot Love You Back?
item.description: What happens when your parent falls in love with an A.I. chatbot? In this episode of “Divided,” Ernie confronts his mother, Celeste, and they try to bridge their differences — and rethink what love could look like.

item.title: As a Tough-on-Crime Prosecutor, I Was Wrong About Harsh Sentencing
item.description: I helped build a punitive criminal justice system. Decades later, this system is failing this country’s aging prisoners. We must set them free.

item.title: Older Women Are in Demand by Younger Men
item.description: What a shift in the dating preferences of younger men reveals about our changing norms.

item.title: MAHA Propelled Kennedy to the White House. It May Become His Downfall.
item.description: MAHA propelled Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to the White House. It may become his downfall.

item.title: What Went Wrong With Criminal Justice Reform
item.description: Americans want a new approach to policing and incarceration.

item.title: This Is Not a Man in Control of Himself
item.description: Trump is in over his head.

item.title: After Mythos, the Future of the Internet Is At a Crossroads
item.description: In the race to patch up cybersecurity holes found by the newest A.I. models, we risk leaving too many people to fend for themselves.

item.title: Pope Leo to Trump: ‘I Have No Fear’
item.description: A harsh attack by the president drew a quick response from the pontiff, a reminder of his uniquely moral voice.

item.title: Vance, the Pope and ‘Matters of Morality’
item.description: Readers respond to Vice President JD Vance’s remarks critical of Pope Leo XIV. Also: Mr. Trump, try kindness; a tax solution that wasn’t.

item.title: Iran Demonstrates the Power of Drones and Cheap Weaponry
item.description: The future of war belongs to “flying lawn mowers”

item.title: Is Caring About Sudan Too Much to Ask?
item.description: I have seen what can happen when Americans care about a wrong in the world.

item.title: Israel’s ‘One-State Reality’
item.description: Hope for a two-state solution between Israel and Palestine is preventing the world from reckoning with the “one-state reality” of the present, the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein argues.

item.title: She Has an A.I. Lover. Her Son Has Questions.
item.description: What happens when your parent falls in love with an A.I. chatbot?

item.title: Trump’s Blasphemy Is a Warning
item.description: Presidents can fight with popes. God is another matter.

item.title: How Trump Can Wrap Up the Iran War
item.description: He can offer the regime a series of fundamental choices.

item.title: Why Aren’t the Kids Out Protesting Against Trump?
item.description: The lack of a thriving youth movement in opposition to Trump is a canary-in-the-coal-mine warning of the deterioration of American exceptionalism.

item.title: Have These People Learned Nothing?
item.description: Why no one is surprised when another member of Congress is accused of sexual misconduct.

item.title: Trump Needs to Get Over the Pope
item.description: Leo XIV isn’t the president’s political rival. He’s God’s messenger.

item.title: Trump, Jesus and the Pope
item.description: Readers respond to front-page articles about President Trump’s erratic behavior.

item.title: Reckoning With Israel’s ‘One-State Reality’
item.description: The political scientists Shibley Telhami and Marc Lynch discuss Israel’s continued expansion into the West Bank, Gaza and Lebanon.

item.title: Here’s How to Defeat Trumpism
item.description: The free world should take an honest look at how Hungary’s opposition party defeated a far-right giant.

item.title: She Has an A.I. Boyfriend. Her Son Has Questions.
item.description: What happens when your parent falls in love with an A.I. chatbot?

item.title: Reckoning With Israel’s ‘One-State Reality’
item.description: The political scientists Shibley Telhami and Marc Lynch discuss Israel’s continued expansion into the West Bank, Gaza and Lebanon.

item.title: ‘I Don’t Want a Person, I Want an A.I.’
item.description: What happens when your parent falls in love with an A.I. chatbot? In this episode of “Divided,” Ernie confronts his mother, Celeste, and they try to bridge their differences — and rethink what love could look like.

item.title: The End of Pax Americana
item.description: America is still a global force, says the Opinion columnist Carlos Lozada. But Trump’s desire to “wield superpowers” without behaving like a superpower is putting its reputation as a reliable leader for peace at risk.
